title: weigela × fujisanensis var. rosea
intro: Weigela × fujisanensis var. rosea, also known as the Fujisan Weigela or Japanese Weigela, is a deciduous shrub native to Japan and China, characterized by its clusters of pink or white flowers that bloom in late spring to early summer. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing, fever reduction, and digestion improvement. The health benefits of Weigela × fujisanensis var. rosea include its potential to lower blood pressure, alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
